reasoning: The informal statement asks to prove that null(T-λI) is *invariant* under S, meaning S(W) ⊆ W (the standard definition of an invariant subspace). The corrected formalization uses `≤` (Submodule containment), which faithfully encodes invariance. The original uses `=`, which asserts the stronger claim that S restricted to W is surjective onto W. This is strictly stronger (equality implies containment, but not vice versa) and is provably false in general: take S = 0, which trivially commutes with T; then Submodule.map S (ker (T - c•id)) = ⊥, which is not equal to ker(T - c•id) whenever T has a nontrivial c-eigenspace. The error is misinterpreting the standard meaning of "invariant under S" as set equality rather than containment — a misunderstanding of the informal statement's terminology.
